---
title: "Concentration and Common Inequalities"
---

## Role

This page is the first bridge from elementary probability into modern theory.

## Why It Matters

Concentration tells you when a random quantity stays close to its mean or another central value. That idea powers learning theory, randomized algorithms, high-dimensional probability, and statistics.

## Core Inequalities To Know

- Markov inequality
- Chebyshev inequality
- Hoeffding-style bounds
- union bound

## Intuition

Expectation tells you the center. Concentration asks how sharply the random object stays near that center.

## Example

If you average many bounded independent random variables, the result typically fluctuates less than a single observation. Concentration inequalities quantify that stability.

## Application Lens

Concentration is often the hidden engine behind claims like:

- empirical averages are reliable
- random projections preserve structure
- generalization error is small with high probability

## Paper Bridge

Once a paper starts saying “with high probability,” you are usually looking at some concentration mechanism, even if it is buried behind a longer theorem.
